title: Deliciously Snappy Polish Bresaola Sandwich
description: A briny and snappy Polish dish featuring bresaola, double-rise-bread, and low-glycemic-friendly ingredients, evoking nostalgic memories of traditional Polish cuisine. This sandwich is perfect for a quick and satisfying snack or lunch.
instructions: Preheat a panini press to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the double-rise-bread into 8 equal slices.
Butter one side of each bread slice.
Layer a slice of bresaola, a few pickle slices, and some chopped fresh dill on the unbuttered side of four bread slices.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Top with the remaining bread slices, buttered side up.
Place the sandwiches in the panini press and cook for 5 minutes, or until the bread is toasted and the filling is heated through.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
